description_long: This town hall is a two-story stone building with a steep, hipped roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The facade features a symmetrical arrangement of windows, with the lower level clad in rusticated stone and the upper level in a lighter, smoother finish. The front entrance is marked by three large arched doorways, above which sits a decorative pediment with ornamental detailing. The roof is punctuated by several chimneys and small dormer windows, adding to the building's stately appearance. The structure is robust and imposing, with classical architectural influences evident in its symmetry and ornamentation. The overall impression is one of civic importance and historical character.
description: A stately, two-story stone town hall with a steep tiled roof, arched entrances, and classical decorative elements. The facade is symmetrical and features ornate detailing above the main entry.
description_view_0: The narrow end of the building is visible, showing the steeply pitched roof, central chimney, and two stories with a single arched entrance below.
description_view_1: The long side of the building is shown, highlighting the symmetrical window arrangement, rusticated lower facade, and tiled roof with two chimneys and dormer windows.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this end view displays the steep roof, central chimney, and the arched entryway, mirroring the opposite side.
description_view_3: The main facade is visible, featuring three large arched entrances, a decorative pediment above, and symmetrical windows on both levels.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Decorative pediment, arched entrances, rusticated stonework, and ornamental cornices
attribute_entrance_design: Three large arched entryways with stone framing, topped by a decorative pediment and flanked by symmetrical windows
